pr.title: ft/ftKirby: decompile Kirby special N and Hi moves
pr.author: johnwinston

summary: Decompiled selected Kirby SpecialN/SpecialHi and copied-Pikachu SpecialN collision helpers across ftKb_SpecialN.c and ftKb_SpecialNNs.c. The diff replaces decomp placeholders with C bodies for Final Cutter startup, inhale swallow proximity checks, and Skull Bash ground/wall collision behavior, plus a narrow dont_inline pragma tactic. Loaded verification is mixed: the PR body claims fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions, while the automated report shows only two 100% new matches and several near-match improvements.

key_files.role: Decompiled Kirby Final Cutter enter functions and inhale swallow proximity helpers; added ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby include and scoped dont_inline pragmas.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialHi_Enter and ftKb_SpecialAirHi_Enter bodies replace placeholders
raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8 and ftKb_SpecialN_800F5EA8 bodies replace placeholders
human_pr_text: summary lists four ftKb_SpecialN.c functions and dont_inline rationale

key_files.role: Decompiled copied-Pikachu Skull Bash collision helpers and wall-bounce callback; added extern ftCollisionBox declaration.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Coll, ftKb_PrSpecialN_Coll, and fn_80105978 bodies replace placeholders
human_pr_text: summary lists three ftKb_SpecialNNs.c functions

what_changed.text: Implemented ftKb_SpecialHi_Enter and ftKb_SpecialAirHi_Enter to clear cmd_vars and specialhi move fields, change to the ground or air Final Cutter startup motion state, run ftAnim_8006EBA4, spawn effect 0x494, and assign efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll hitlag callbacks.
Implemented ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8 for inhaled item checks using Kirby position plus dat_attrs mouth offsets, it_802F23AC distance, item swallow call it_802F2810, and ground/air transition split.
Implemented ftKb_SpecialN_800F5EA8 for inhaled fighter checks using the same offset-position pattern, ftCo_800BD19C distance, ftCo_800BD620 capture/wait handling, and ground/air transition split.
Added scoped #pragma dont_inline blocks around ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8 and the capture animation callers; the PR body states this prevents MWCC from inlining 800F5DE8 into callers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: #pragma dont_inline on/off around ftKb_SpecialNCapture1_Anim and ftKb_SpecialAirNCapture_Anim

what_changed.text: Implemented ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Coll to detect landing, switch to ground copied-Pikachu Skull Bash start/loop state based on mv.pr.specialn.x34.x, and restore callback fields.

Implemented ftKb_PrSpecialN_Coll to detect wall collision via ft_800824A0 and ftKb_Init_803CB758, change to ftKb_MS_PrSpecialNEndR, restore callback fields, and transfer horizontal self velocity into ground velocity while clearing self_vel y/z.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: extern ftKb_Init_803CB758 and ftKb_PrSpecialN_Coll body

what_changed.text: Implemented fn_80105978 as a wall-bounce helper that rotates the model and negates self_vel.x, gr_vel, ground acceleration, multiple mv.pr.specialn fields, facing_dir, and x34 vector components.
Made formatting-only line wrapping changes in fn_800F98F4 around it_802C01AC and efSync_Spawn calls; the existing PAD_STACK(4) was not introduced by this PR excerpt.

smart_moves.text: The PR used existing typed fighter structures and move-specific union fields such as fp->mv.kb.specialhi and fp->mv.pr.specialn rather than raw offset arithmetic in the newly decompiled bodies.

The Final Cutter enter implementations reacquire Fighter* after Fighter_ChangeMotionState/ftAnim_8006EBA4 before writing callback fields, preserving a local source shape visible in the diff rather than assuming the old pointer remains the intended expression.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fp = GET_FIGHTER(gobj) after ftAnim_8006EBA4 in both enter functions

smart_moves.text: The inhale item and fighter helpers share a clear local pattern: compute mouth-relative position from dat_attrs, compare squared distance against squared inhale velocity, then branch to ground or air transition.
The wall-bounce helper keeps an address-style name, fn_80105978, while the PR body describes behavior; this avoids promoting an unsupported semantic symbol rename.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For fighter move enter functions, matching source may require clearing command variables and move-state fields before Fighter_ChangeMotionState, then restoring effect hitlag callbacks after animation/effect setup.

Inhale/capture proximity helpers can be expressed with typed dat_attrs offsets from cur_pos and a squared-distance comparison against a squared velocity/radius threshold, with separate ground and air state transitions.

Copied-character collision code may need to restore standard fighter callback fields immediately after changing motion state; this PR repeats death2_cb, take_dmg_cb, deal_dmg_cb, and x21F8 assignments in both landing and wall-collision paths.
When a helper's semantic purpose is understood but the symbol name is still address-style, keep the address-style symbol unless there is stronger naming evidence.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: describes it as wall-bounce helper

naming_conventions.text: Kirby fighter functions in this slice use the ftKb_ prefix and descriptive move/state suffixes such as SpecialHi_Enter, SpecialAirHi_Enter, PrSpecialAirNStart_Coll, and PrSpecialN_Coll.
Unknown or not-yet-semantically-renamed helpers may remain address-style, as with fn_80105978, even when the PR body explains its behavior.
Local Fighter pointers use short role names fp and fp2; dat_attrs is shortened to da, and local position vectors use pos.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Scoped #pragma dont_inline was used around ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8 and around the caller animation functions to influence MWCC inlining behavior.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: prevents MWCC from inlining 800F5DE8 into callers
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Global standards treat pragmas as exceptions requiring narrow verifier evidence. The loaded report does not show ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8 as a full 100% match, so this should not be promoted as a general standard from this PR alone.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: The PR uses typed fields and existing callbacks rather than M2C_FIELD or raw pointer math in the decompiled functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: typed fp->cmd_vars, fp->mv.kb.specialhi, fp->mv.pr.specialn, fp->pre_hitlag_cb, fp->post_hitlag_cb
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: This is consistent with loaded standards but the PR evidence does not prove why each field name was chosen.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: The Final Cutter ground and air enter functions are nearly parallel but preserve different command-variable assignment shapes: sequential reverse order in ground and chained assignment in air.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: No review or verifier note explains whether this difference was required for matching.

review_feedback.text: No human review comments are loaded for this PR slice.

Automated report by encounter showed overall matched code 59.41% (+0.01%, +368 bytes), with ftKb_SpecialHi_Enter and ftKb_SpecialAirHi_Enter newly reaching 100%. It also showed several functions improved but not fully matched, contradicting or narrowing the PR body's broad 'fuzzy_match_percent: 100.0 for all functions' claim.

follow_up_queries: Search later ftKirby PRs for cleanup or full match of ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8 after dont_inline use.
Search for other uses of #pragma dont_inline in fighter move code and whether verifier reports justify them.
Search ftKb_SpecialNNs.c history for semantic rename or further documentation of fn_80105978.
Search for ftKb_Init_803CB758 declarations to confirm owning header or data symbol placement.
Search for copied-Pikachu Kirby Skull Bash collision functions to compare callback restoration patterns.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: merged PR metadata and changed file counts
human_pr_text: PR body summary and functional descriptions
raw_diff: complete diff excerpt for two files
notes: Changed files and diff hunks are strong evidence for what source changed.
Functional descriptions are from the PR body and align with visible code, but no reviewer discussion confirms intent.

Verification evidence is inconsistent: the PR body claims fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions, but the loaded automated report records only two 100% new matches and lists the other decompiled functions as 91.96% to 99.96% improvements.
There are no loaded review comments or approval notes, so reviewer intent and final acceptance rationale are not inferable beyond merged state.
The dont_inline tactic is explicitly stated by the author and visible in the diff, but the loaded report does not prove it produced a full match for the affected helper.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Typed move fields for Kirby inhale and copied-Pikachu collision helpers
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2322 expresses new Kirby SpecialN/SpecialHi code with existing typed Fighter fields and move-specific union fields rather than raw offsets, including fp->mv.kb.specialhi and fp->mv.pr.specialn. Useful as supporting evidence for typed-field recovery in ftKirby move code.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep address-style helper names when semantics are described but not symbol-backed
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2322 describes fn_80105978 as a wall-bounce helper but leaves the symbol as fn_80105978. This is useful supporting evidence for conservative naming in decomp PRs.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider documenting narrow dont_inline evidence requirements with PR #2322 as cautionary example
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2322 used scoped #pragma dont_inline to prevent MWCC from inlining ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8 into callers. The tactic is visible and author-stated, but loaded automated verification only shows 91.96% for ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8 and near-matches for several functions, not full proof for all claims. If standards are expanded, frame this as a cautionary example: keep dont_inline tightly scoped and require narrow objdiff/checkdiff evidence.
source_update_candidates.reason: The existing standards already cover pragma caution; this PR may provide a concrete historical example but should not weaken the evidence requirement.

source_update_candidates.title: ftKirby SpecialN files contain Kirby inhale, Final Cutter, and copied-Pikachu Skull Bash logic
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2322 changed src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialN.c for Final Cutter startup and inhale swallow checks, and src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNNs.c for copied-Pikachu Skull Bash collision helpers.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could improve source-path search and subsystem routing, but should be curated as a path fact rather than inferred globally.

rejection_notes: Do not record 'all functions matched 100%' as accepted verification; the loaded automated report contradicts the PR body for several functions.
Do not promote dont_inline as normal decomp style from this PR; global standards require narrow evidence for pragmas and matching tactics.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale, because no review comments or reviews are loaded.
Do not semantically rename fn_80105978 from this evidence alone; the PR retained the address-style name.
